title: Quantum Leap Innovation Fest 2025
tagline: Exploring the intersection of quantum and classical computing
description: Quantum Leap brought together 320 participants to experiment with quantum algorithms, hybrid classical-quantum workflows, and quantum-inspired optimization. This closed event featured IBM Quantum and AWS Braket credits, workshops on Qiskit and Cirq, and challenges in cryptography, logistics, and drug discovery simulation. Winning teams presented at Q2B 2025.
rules: Quantum circuits must run on provided simulators or partner hardware. Hybrid solutions encouraged. Teams of 2–4. All submissions required reproducible notebooks.
eligibility: Open to researchers and students with linear algebra background. Introductory quantum workshops were provided pre-event.

tracks.name: Quantum Machine Learning
tracks.description: Hybrid quantum-classical ML models for classification and generative tasks.
tracks.problemStatement: Implement a variational quantum classifier on a provided dataset and compare accuracy vs. classical baselines.

tracks.name: Optimization & Simulation
tracks.description: Quantum-inspired algorithms for combinatorial optimization problems.
tracks.problemStatement: Solve a vehicle routing problem using QAOA or quantum annealing. Benchmark against classical heuristics.

tracks.name: Quantum DevTools
tracks.description: Tools that simplify quantum circuit design, debugging, and visualization.
tracks.problemStatement: Build a circuit debugger that visualizes qubit states step-by-step and highlights decoherence hotspots.

tracks.name: Quantum-Safe Cryptography
tracks.description: Post-quantum cryptographic schemes and blockchain integration.
tracks.problemStatement: Implement a post-quantum signature scheme and demonstrate a simple blockchain block signed with lattice-based cryptography.

timeline.label: Hackathon Begins
timeline.description: IBM Quantum and Braket credits distributed.

timeline.label: Judging
timeline.description: Expert panel reviews quantum advantage claims.

faqs.question: Do I need quantum hardware access?
faqs.answer: Simulators were sufficient. Hardware access was optional via IBM and AWS credits.

faqs.question: What background is required?
faqs.answer: Linear algebra and basic Python. Pre-event workshops covered quantum fundamentals.

faqs.question: Could teams use PennyLane?
faqs.answer: Yes. Qiskit, Cirq, PennyLane, and Braket SDKs were all supported.

faqs.question: How were results verified?
faqs.answer: Judges re-ran notebooks on provided infrastructure to verify claims.

faqs.question: Were there qubit limits?
faqs.answer: Simulator shots unlimited. Hardware jobs capped at 1000 shots per team.

faqs.question: Is this event recurring?
faqs.answer: Quantum Leap 2026 is planned for Q4 2026. Join the mailing list for updates.
